heards.headline: Refidomsa seeks up to 1.75 mil barrels gasoil for delivery May-July or October
heards.body: Buyer: Refidomsa Country: Dominican Republic Specifications & quantity: A minimum of 944,590 barrels of gasoil to be delivered over three months, or 1.756 million barrels of gasoil to be delivered over six months. Specifications call for a maximum sulfur content of 0.20% of mass, a minimum cetane index of 45, and a maximum sediment content of 0.01% of mass. Port: Berth 1 and 2 Haina Occidental, Rio Haina on DAT basis Delivery: First option calls for 944,590 barrels delivered May-July. The second option is 1.756 million barrels delivered May-October, each option plus or minus 10% at Refidomsa’s option. Price: A differential to Platts Gasoil No. 2 USGC waterborne midpoint (POAEE00) Close: April 9, with validity until April 15.

stories.headline: Platts US Gulf Coast Export Crude Daily Commentary
stories.summary: WTI FOB USGC falls $8.80/b, assessed at $95.27/b Crude futures fall as US, Iran signal willingness to resume talks
stories.body: US crude export prices weakened along with global crude futures on April 14 as the US and Iran signaled willingness to resume peace talks. Platts assessed West Texas Intermediate loading on an FOB basis along the US Gulf Coast $8.80/barrel weaker to end the day at $95.27/b on an outright basis. WTI FOB USGC was assessed $4.22/b weaker against the NYMEX WTI strip at a $8.02/b premium. WTI FOB USGC was assessed $2.88/b cents/b weaker against the Dated Brent strip at a $4.18/b discount. There were two trades for WTI Midland on a CIF Rotterdam basis during the Platts North Sea Market on Close assessment process. TotalEnergies bought a May 14-18 cargo from Phillips 66 for $22.80/b over Dated Brent and Moeve bought a May 11-15 cargo from Chevron for $21.85/b above Dated Brent. Platts assessed the Dirty USGC-UKC Aframax rate at $84.04/metric ton, down $10.51/mt on the day. The rate has fallen by $89.29/mt since March 30. NYMEX May WTI ended the session down $7.80/barrel at $91.28/b and ICE June Brent declined $4.57/b to $94.79/b. Platts is part of S&P Global Energy.

stories.headline: Platts US Pipeline Crude Daily Commentary
stories.summary: Crude futures slide as US, Iran signal willingness to resume talks Mars falls $4.25/b, assessed at $2.25/b premium WTI MEH down 95 cents/b, assessed at $2.25/b premium
stories.body: US Gulf Coast crude oil differentials plunged April 14 as demand destruction brought markets closer to balance. Platts assessed Mars medium sour $4.25/barrel weaker at a $2.25/b premium to cash WTI. Platts assessed West Texas Intermediate in Midland, Texas, at a $1.30/b premium to cash WTI, 90 cents/b weaker. Platts assessed WTI at the Magellan East Houston terminal 95 cents/b weaker at a $2.25/b premium to cash WTI. WTI Midland and WTI MEH differentials have fallen $3.20/b and $5.50/b, respectively, since April 2. Global crude futures weakened amid reports that Tehran and Washington have signaled a willingness to resume peace talks. NYMEX May WTI ended the session down $7.80/b at $91.28/b, and ICE June Brent declined $4.57/b to $94.79/b. Platts is part of S&P Global Energy.

stories.headline: Platts USAC Jet Barge, Pipe and Gasoil No.2 Daily Rationales and Exclusions
stories.body: Jet Kero New Jersey Buckeye Pipeline <<AAAAJNL00> assessment rationale: Platts assessed jet fuel on Buckeye Pipeline on April 14 based on a trade at NYMEX May ULSD at plus 24.00 cents/gallon heard in the latest Platts Market on Close assessment process. Jet Kero New York Harbor Barge <<PJPJAAW00> assessment rationale: Platts assessed New York jet fuel barges April 14 based on an established relationship to Buckeye Pipeline. There were no positions in the Platts Market on Close assessment process. Gasoil No. 2 New York Harbor Barge <<POPOAEG00> assessment rationale: Platts assessed US Atlantic Coast No. 2 gasoil barges April 14 based on an established relationship to US Gulf Coast pipeline gasoil. There were no positions in the Platts Market on Close assessment process. Platts is a part of S&P Global Energy. Exclusions: There were no exclusions in the April 14 assessment process.

stories.headline: Platts USGC Jet 54 and Gasoil No.2 Daily Rationales and Exclusions
stories.body: Jet Kero 54 USGC Prompt Pipeline <<PJPJABO00> assessment rationale: Platts assessed US Gulf Coast jet for Colonial Pipeline’s prompt cycle on April 14 based on an offer at NYMEX May ULSD plus 18.00 cents/gallon seen in the latest Platts Market on Close assessment process. Gasoil No. 2 USGC Prompt Pipeline <<POAPOAED00> assessment rationale: Platts assessed US Gulf Coast No. 2 gasoil for Colonial Pipeline's prompt cycle April 14 based on the latest heard relationship to ultra-low sulfur diesel for Colonial Pipeline and the Renewable Volume Obligation cost. Platts is a part of S&P Global Energy. Exclusions: There were no exclusions in the April 14 assessment process.

stories.headline: Demand destruction pressures USGC crude differentials
stories.summary: USGC crude differentials plunge on demand loss Freight rates drop $89/mt since late March peak IEA cuts 2026 oil demand forecast by 720,000 b/d
stories.body: US Gulf Coast crude oil differentials plummeted April 14 as demand destruction has brought the market closer to balance after a prolonged period of elevated prices and freight rates. Platts assessed both West Texas Intermediate in Houston and Mars medium sour at premiums of $2.25/b above cash WTI, day-over-day declines of 95 cents/b and $4.25/b, respectively. They are each grade’s lowest assessed values since March 24 and March 11, respectively. Platts assessed the Dirty USGC-UKC Aframax rate at $84.04/mt, down $10.51/mt day over day. The rate has fallen by $89.29/mt since March 30. “High freight [and] high diffs go hand in hand on physical price,” a US-based analyst said. The combination of reduced Middle Eastern production and drawdowns from oil-on-water inventories and strategic petroleum reserves has made “the global balance net flat,” the source said. A second source, a US-based trader, said demand destruction was particularly acute in Asia. Global oil demand will contract by 80,000 barrels/day in 2026 due to demand destruction from the Middle East war, the International Energy Agency said April 14. The IEA now expects global demand to average 104.26 million b/d over 2026, down from 104.34 million b/d in 2025. In March, the IEA predicted global demand would rise by 640,000 b/d in 2026. Also, the IEA has revised down its global refinery runs in April due to the Middle East conflict. The IEA estimates Middle Eastern crude runs 3 million b/d lower and another 2.7 million b/d in Asia. Platts is part of S&P Global Energy.
